question: What's the best time to visit Stanthorpe QLD with kids?
answer: G'day! Autumn (March-May) is absolutely brilliant for families - the weather's mild, perfect for exploring with little ones. Spring (September-November) is gorgeous too, with wildflowers blooming everywhere. Winter gets pretty chilly (great for cosy fires!), while summer can be warm but pleasant compared to coastal areas. School holidays book out fast year-round, so get in early if you're planning a visit during peak times!

question: Are there pet-friendly holiday parks in Stanthorpe QLD?
answer: You bet! Several holiday parks around Stanthorpe welcome your furry family members. Top of the Granite Belt and Stanthorpe Holiday Park both offer pet-friendly accommodation options. Just make sure you book ahead and mention you're bringing your four-legged mate - there are usually specific pet-friendly cabins or sites. Most require pets to be kept on leads and have size or breed restrictions, so double-check when booking.

question: How much do holiday parks cost in Stanthorpe QLD during school holidays?
answer: During school holidays, expect to pay around $150-$280 per night for a family cabin sleeping 4-6 people. Powered caravan sites are more budget-friendly at $45-$65 per night. Unpowered sites start from about $35-$45. Prices jump during peak times like Easter and Christmas holidays, so booking a few months ahead usually snags you better rates and availability. Midweek stays are often cheaper than weekends too!

question: What should I pack for a holiday park stay with young kids?
answer: Essential packing list: layered clothing (mornings and evenings get cold!), comfy walking shoes, sunscreen, hats, and insect repellent. Don't forget bikes, scooters, or outdoor toys - kids love exploring holiday parks. Pack your own linen if staying budget-style, plus basic pantry items. A first aid kit, night light, and favourite comfort toys are lifesavers. Oh, and bring board games for cosy evenings in!

question: What is there to do near Stanthorpe QLD holiday parks for families?
answer: Heaps of fun stuff! Visit family-friendly wineries with playgrounds (yes, really!), go fruit picking seasonally, explore Girraween National Park's stunning granite boulders and easy walking tracks, or check out the Stanthorpe Heritage Museum. Kids love feeding animals at local farms, swimming at Storm King Dam, and fossil hunting. Many holiday parks have playgrounds, jumping pillows, and games rooms too - entertainment sorted!
